description: Realm allocated during incremental GC swept as dead before it could be marked. CVE-2022-45406 (sec-high, MFSA 2022-47, Firefox 107). Compartment::sweepRealms in js/src/gc/GC.cpp assumed every realm in the compartment existed at the start of the current incremental collection. A newGlobal/Reflect.parse/OOM path can allocate and link a new JS::Realm in js/src/vm/Realm.cpp while the GC is already marking or sweeping; because the fresh realm has no marked global yet, sweepRealms destroys it. Later compaction still reaches BaseShape/shape metadata that references the freed Realm via BaseShape::realm(), and Realm::unsafeUnbarrieredMaybeGlobal() dereferences poisoned memory. The fix adds Realm::allocatedDuringIncrementalGC_ state, preserves realms allocated during an active GC in Realm::hasLiveGlobalPreserveColor, and clears the flag at collection boundaries so those realms are only eligible for sweeping in a later GC cycle.

fixes.description: Bug 1791975 - Don't sweep realms that were allocated during
